data.title: Palo Alto Networks slides following Q1 results, acquisition of Chronosphere
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks' (PANW) released its first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results post-market on Wednesday, which topped market estimates.

Shares were down 4% during early post-market trading.

The company also announced the acquisition of Chronosphere for $3.35B. The deal is expected to close during the second half of 2026. The software company, which was founded in New York City in 2019, provides a cloud-native observability platform designed to help DevOps and site reliability engineering teams monitor and manage the performance and reliability of complex, containerized applications and infrastructure.

For the quarter ended October 31, Palo Alto reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.93, which was more than the consensus estimate of $0.89. GAAP EPS was $0.47 versus the estimate of $0.44.

Revenue for the first quarter increased 16% year over year to total $2.5B, which was more than the $2.46B estimate.

Looking ahead, Palo Alto projects second-quarter revenue to range from $2.57B to $2.59B, with a midpoint directly in line with the $2.58B estimate. The company expects adjusted EPS to range from $0.93 to $0.95 versus the $0.93 estimate.

For the entirety of 2026, Palo Alto increased its revenue forecast to range from $10.5B to $10.54B versus the estimate of $10.51B. The company previously expected revenue to range from $10.48B to $10.53B.

"This sustained profitable growth was reflected by another quarter of 30% plus operating margin and reinforces our confidence in achieving 40% plus adjusted free cash flow margin in FY'28," said Palo Alto CFO Dipak Golechha.

"Our robust innovation engine, paired with the strategic acquisitions of CyberArk and Chronosphere, positions us as the data and security partner of choice in the AI era," said Palo Alto CEO Nikesh Arora.

In the most recent quarter, Chronosphere generated more than $160M in annual recurring revenue, which represented a triple-digit increase year over year.

In July, Palo Alto announced the planned acquisition of CyberArk (CYBR) for $25B. CyberArk provides identity security and privileged access management.

Palo Alto also announced the appointment of Mark Goodburn to its board of directors and the retirement of Mary Pat McCarthy, who has served on the board since 2016. Her retirement date is set for Jan. 23, 2026.

Goodburn served as the Chairman and Global Head of Advisory of KPMG International from 2011 to 2021.

data.title: Genco Shipping & Trading acquires Newcastlemax vessels for $145.5M
data.description:
• Genco Shipping & Trading (NYSE:GNK) announced on Wednesday that it has agreed to acquire two 2020-built 208,000 dwt scrubber-fitted Newcastlemax vessels for a total purchase price of $145.5M.
  • The company expects to take delivery of the vessels during the first quarter of 2026 and intends to fund the acquisition with cash on hand and a drawdown from its revolving credit facility.
  • Pursuant to this agreement, the company's investment in modern fuel-efficient Capesize and Newcastlemax tonnage will total $343 million.
  • This acquisition is a strategic investment to further align the company's capital deployment with their thesis on the dry bulk market and specifically the Capesize and Newcastlemax sector.

data.title: Nvidia pops as AI strength buoys Q3 results, guidance; Huang says cloud GPUs 'sold out'
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) shares rose 2.5% in extended trading on Wednesday after the semiconductor giant posted third-quarter results and guidance that were above Wall Street's forecast amid insatiable demand for artificial intelligence processors.

For the period ending Oct. 26, the Jensen Huang-led company said it earned $1.30 per share as revenue soared 62% year-over-year to come in at $66.01B. Analysts had expected the company to earn $1.26 per share on $55.19B in revenue.

Data center revenue jumped 66% year-over-year, coming in at $51.2B, above the $49.34B estimate. Automotive revenue for the period clocked in at $592M, up 32% year-over-year. Professional visualization revenue rose 56% year-over-year to $760M. Gaming revenue for the period was $4.3B, up 30% year-over-year, but below the $4.42B estimate. Adjusted gross margin came in at 73.6% for the quarter. 

“Blackwell sales are off the charts, and cloud GPUs are sold out,” said Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of NVIDIA. “Compute demand keeps accelerating and compounding across training and inference — each growing exponentially. We’ve entered the virtuous cycle of AI. The AI ecosystem is scaling fast — with more new foundation model makers, more AI startups, across more industries, and in more countries. AI is going everywhere, doing everything, all at once.”

Looking to the fourth-quarter of fiscal 2026, Nvidia expects to generate $65B in revenue, plus or minus 2%. Analysts were forecasting $61.98B in revenue. GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins are expected to be 74.8% and 75.0%, respectively, plus or minus 50 basis points.

Joe Albano, Investing Group Leader for Tech Cache, said the results and guidance are further proof that Nvidia is the "kingpin" of AI. 

"If Nvidia keeps beating and raising, the party essentially keeps going," Albano said via email. "And Nvidia didn't disappoint this quarter either, with another beat and another serious guide above consensus - all without China." 

Victor Dergunov, Investing Group Leader for The Financial Prophet, agreed. "[This is a] stellar report from Nvidia, signalling that the AI market will likely continue to inflate and the recent declines in AI and momentum stocks could be buying opportunities," Dergunov said via email.

"This dynamic illustrates that the AI market is not near topping, despite this transitory delationary phase, potentially greenlighting a year-end rally for top AI stocks and other high-quality names," Dergunov added.

In conjunction with the results, Nvidia said it would pay its quarterly dividend of $0.01 per share to shareholders on Dec. 26, to all shareholders of record on Dec. 4. 

The company will host a conference call at 5 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

(This story has been updated with analyst commentary.)

data.title: New Jersey Resources Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 misses by $0.03, revenue of $336.08M beats by $36.33M
data.description:
• New Jersey Resources press release (NJR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 misses by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $336.08M (-15.1% Y/Y) beats by $36.33M.

  • Fiscal 2026 Outlook

  • Maintains 7 to 9 percent long-term net financial earnings per share (NFEPS) growth target, starting from a fiscal 2025 base of $2.83 per share
  • Introduces fiscal 2026 NFEPS guidance range of $3.03 to $3.18

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Nvidia sees FQ4 revenue above estimates after solid FQ3 print
Hillenbrand Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.83, revenue of $652M
data.description:
• Hillenbrand press release (HI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.83.
  • Revenue of $652M.
  • FY 2025 net revenue of $2.67 billion decreased 16% vs. prior year; pro forma net revenue decreased 9%
